title: Avoid git warnings by using detach on checkout
body:
Git displays a huge and confusing warning when doing a checkout of
a specific commit if the --detach option is not mentioned.

This cleans up our build logs, making it easier to spot real problems.

comments.body: Could you do a `make vendor-in-container` and repush.

> Could you do a `make vendor-in-container` and repush.

Sadly this does not really work reliably. On one platform it works without changing anything, on another one (rhel8) I cannot use `vendor-in-container` because it complains about an outdated conman. 

The big question is how to reproduce the context that causes this on that particular CI job. I kinda find ironic that this specific make target proves to be less portable than the others. My guess that it was created especially for get reproducible results.
